issue.title: D: Single source of truth — deriveDisplayStatus() selector

successCriteria.description: deriveDisplayStatus() implements all 8 first-match precedence rows from #218 (incl. anomaly surfacing)
Kanban column placement, detail GitHub badge, and pipeline timeline all consume the selector (no independent status derivation remains on those surfaces)
deriveDisplayStatus.test.ts passes with the 6 mandated fixtures (216-mid-cascade, release-child-shipped, reopened, closed-not-merged, failed, paused) + a determinism check
A grep guard test fails on inline ?? [] / ?? {} / || [] in selector/hook-arg positions (guards the #210/#212 fresh-ref crash class)

phases.title: Author the pure selector deriveDisplayStatus.ts + the 8-row precedence table + 6 fixtures
phases.content: Create `apps/desktop/src/utils/deriveDisplayStatus.ts` (sibling of `githubRefreshTriggers.ts`): a pure function, no React, no Zustand imports, minimal structural input interfaces. Implements the canonical first-match precedence table from #218. Returns `{ column, label, pipelineState, badge, anomaly? }`. Author the file directly (implementation, not design — spec is fixed in #218). Create `apps/desktop/src/utils/__tests__/deriveDisplayStatus.test.ts` with the 6 mandated fixtures + a referential-determinism check (same input ⇒ deep-equal output).
verification: deriveDisplayStatus.ts exports deriveDisplayStatus + DisplayStatus/DisplayColumn/PipelineState/DisplayBadge types + the minimal input interfaces (WorkLike, GithubStateLike, HistoryLike)
All 8 precedence rows implemented in first-match order; row 1 (tiki done/shipping + GitHub open) sets an anomaly and pipelineState 'active' (NOT 'complete')
deriveDisplayStatus.test.ts has the 6 named fixtures + determinism test; vitest green
phases.summary: Authored apps/desktop/src/utils/deriveDisplayStatus.ts (pure, no React/Zustand) implementing all 8 #218 precedence rows; 11 tests incl. 6 mandated fixtures + determinism check, green.

phases.title: Refactor Kanban column placement, detail badge, and pipeline timeline to consume the selector
phases.content: KanbanBoard.tsx: replace the inline statusToColumn + getIssueColumn + per-column activeWork/GitHub branching with deriveDisplayStatus(...).column (keep the completedColumn.ts union for the Completed card synthesis + exclusion — that is the #219 fix). IssueDetail.tsx: derive the GitHub badge via deriveDisplayStatus(...).badge instead of reading issue.state directly. PipelineTimeline.tsx: add a pipelineState prop; getStepState consumes it (complete=all green; active/failed/paused cap at currentStep; reset=all pending; partial=all green except SHIP). Wire IssueDetail to pass deriveDisplayStatus(...).pipelineState. All call sites memoized (useMemo) keyed on inputs — no inline fresh refs.
verification: grep: KanbanBoard.tsx no longer contains a standalone statusToColumn switch for column placement (routes through deriveDisplayStatus)
IssueDetail badge text derives from deriveDisplayStatus(...).badge
PipelineTimeline renders from pipelineState; #216-style mid-cascade item shows NOT-all-green + Open badge
phases.summary: KanbanBoard column placement, IssueDetail GitHub badge, and PipelineTimeline now consume the selector. Anomaly surfaced in detail panel. #219 completed-column union untouched.

phases.title: Add the fresh-ref grep guard test + full build/test verification
phases.content: Add `apps/desktop/src/utils/__tests__/freshRefGuard.test.ts` (env node) that scans the selector + consuming-surface + store-selector files and fails on inline `?? []` / `?? {}` / `|| []` outside module-scope `EMPTY_`/`const` declarations (the #210/#212 crash class). Run pnpm build + pnpm test; fix any fallout.
verification: freshRefGuard.test.ts present and passing; deliberately introducing an inline `?? []` in a scanned file makes it fail
phases.summary: Added freshRefGuard.test.ts; full desktop suite 168/168, pnpm build (tsc -b) clean.
